description: A long slender unit of natural or manufactured material is 'linua', especially one that can be spun, twisted, felted, or bonded with others. Fibres may come from plants, animals, minerals, or synthetic production. 'sima' (thread) is a continuous strand made from fibres or a filament.
articulatory_notes: Lateral 'l' releases into high-front 'i'. Dental nasal 'n' begins the stressed syllable behind the upper teeth and opens into rounded 'u', followed by a clean hiatus into final 'a'.
sound_symbolism: The opening 'li' is shared with 'liru' (fabric) and 'lina' (instrument string). Those related forms sit close in the ear, then each leaves the shared opening by a different route.
usage_notes: One fibre can be counted with optional 'themo'. Its material and length help identify it; a practical account can add treatment or hazard when relevant.

semantic_domains.creation: a slender material unit used in thread, fabric, paper, and composite materials
semantic_domains.physical: a long thin natural or manufactured material element
